cot.content: The subplot for CBF Value is shown on the bottom row of the figure. Time(s) is shown on the horizontal axis and CBF Value is shown on the vertical axis. Each interval on the vertical axis represents 3 units.
The legend above the subplots shows that the values for nominal dynamics are shown by the orange line and the values for learned dynamics are shown by the blue line.
The time 3s sits halfway between the tick marks for 2 and 4 on the horizontal axis.
At time 3s, the corresponding CBF value on the orange line is approximately 8 as it lies 2/3 of the way between the tick marks for 0 and 12 on the vertical axis.

At time 3s, the corresponding CBF value on the blue line is approximately 16 as because the next tick mark above 12 represents 15 and this value lies just above the next tick mark above 12 on the vertical axis.
The difference between these values is 16-8 = 8.
At time equals 3s the difference between the CBF value for the line representing learned dynamics and the CBF value for the line representing nominal dynamics is 8.
